input.headline: I do get jealous and question whether I’m cut out for non-monogamy

raw.headline: I do get jealous and question whether I’m cut out for non-monogamy

winner: I asked my therapist if I was cut out for non-monogamy, and she said, "Well, since you're technically only one person, you're overqualified for the job.

candidates.text: I told my partner I was considering polyamory, and now I'm the one questioning if I'm cut out for jealousy.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the original headline with concise, specific humor that plays on irony and self-awareness."}

candidates.text: I tried non-monogamy, but when my partner asked if I was "cut out for it," I checked the box labeled "Jealousy" and left the rest unchecked.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a clever twist on the headline with a relatable scenario."}

candidates.text: I never thought I was cut out for monogamy—turns out my problem is just that I have a *mono*-gaming habit; I’ll play one game at a time, thanks.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'mono' and 'non-monogamy,' concise and surprising with a clear connection to the headline."}

candidates.text: I'm not cut out for non-monogamy either—every time my partner leaves the room, I start rearranging all their furniture to make them feel less welcome when they return.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on jealousy with a specific, relatable scenario that's both funny and cleverly worded."}

candidates.text: I do get jealous and question whether I'm cut out for non-monogamy — but at least I know I'm not a polyamorous potato.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison with a polyamorous potato adds humor while staying concise and relevant to the headline."}

candidates.text: I asked my therapist if I was cut out for non-monogamy, and she said, "Well, since you're technically only one person, you're overqualified for the job.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on the concept of being 'overqualified' for non-monogamy. It uses natural English and effectively ties the therapist's response to the headline in a humorous way."}

candidates.text: I’m not sure I’m cut out for non-monogamy because I’m starting to get jealous of my husband's commitment to my emotional well-being.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild twist, but the humor is subtle and may not land as strongly due to the complex setup involving non-monogamy and emotional well-being."}

candidates.text: I’m starting to suspect I’m not cut out for non-monogamy; I just can't handle having any competition for my own feelings.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relatable and has a mild twist, but it lacks the sharp surprise or specificity that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}

candidates.text: Why did the monogamous person bring a ladder to their relationship? Because they always wanted to *climb* back on!
